page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor operating at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 32 physical cores with a single thread per core. The CPU allocation is shared, which represents a potential performance tradeoff during periods of high host contention. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and AES cryptography extensions.

The system provides a high memory density of 8.0 GB per core, totaling 256.0 GB of RAM. It includes 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core CPU stress tests and large-block memory bandwidth, both reading and writing. Multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Passmark CPU tests fall into the strong tier. Database operations and static web serving throughput register in the average tier, while Redis SET operations achieve strong performance at 71.28 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. The combination of 256.0 GB of RAM and 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes it highly suitable for large-scale caching, memory-mapped databases, and data processing pipelines. It is also well-suited for medium-sized language model inference, where text generation benchmarks show strong performance, as well as general-purpose enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio. It is powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture with a shared CPU allocation. The server includes 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core and cryptography execution. While database operations and web serving throughput perform at average levels, Redis SET operations are strong. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, data processing, and medium-scale database deployments where shared CPU resources align with qualitative cost-efficiency goals.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is a memory-optimized server with 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3200 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong database performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3200 GB NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors and high-capacity local NVMe storage for demanding data workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
3200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for memory-mapped databases, caching, and data processing
